title: Raja Ram Mohan Roy
provisions: Known as pioneer of Indian socio-religious reforms.
Wrote Persian text Tuhfat-ul-Muwahhidin (Gift to Monotheists).
Helped in abolition of Sati Pratha (1829).

title: Debendranath Tagore
provisions: Objective was to carry forward Raja Ram Mohan Roy’s legacy.
Became critical of Christian missionary conversion activities.

title: Keshab Chandra Sen
provisions: Called for respect for all religions.
Controversy over marriage of his minor daughter to Prince of Cooch Behar.

title: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar
provisions: Refined rules of modern Bengali prose.
Dedicated life to upliftment of women.
Served as Secretary of Bethune School.
Served as Principal of Sanskrit College, Bengal.
Persuaded British authorities for Hindu Widow Remarriage Act (1856).

title: Dayanand Saraswati
provisions: Gave the call India for Indians.
Gave mantra Go back to the Vedas.
Objective was reconversion from Islam and Christianity.

title: Swami Vivekananda
provisions: Participated in World’s Parliament of Religions (Chicago, 1893).
Called for balance between spiritualism of East and materialism of West.
Not directly involved in political activities.
Infused youth with pride in India’s past.

title: Annie Besant
provisions: Advocated education based on Indian ideals.
Institution later became Banaras Hindu University.

title: M. G. Ranade
provisions: Wrote A Theist’s Confession of Faith.
Supported increase in age of marriage.
Early leader associated with Indian National Congress.

title: Jyotiba Phule
provisions: Established girls’ school at Bhidewada, Pune.

title: Pandita Ramabai
provisions: Provided asylum and vocational training to women.
Case led to Age of Consent Act (1891).

title: Henry Vivian Derozio
provisions: Inspired by ideals of the French Revolution.

title: Ramakrishna Paramhansa
provisions: Gave message Service to mankind is service to God.
Gave mantra Yatra Jiva Tatra Shiva.

title: Swami Hansraj
provisions: Promoted education combining Vedic knowledge and Western science.

title: Swami Shraddhanand
provisions: Promoted education based on traditional Indian learning.

title: Sree Narayana Guru
provisions: Initiator of Sree Narayana Dharma Paripalana Yogam (1901).
Gave mantra One caste, one God, one religion.

title: T. M. Nair
provisions: Founder of South Indian Liberal Federation (Justice Party).
Objective was upliftment of backward classes.
Objective was political representation for non-Brahmins.

title: P. Thyagaraya Chetty
provisions: Founder of South Indian Liberal Federation (Justice Party).
Objective was upliftment of backward classes.
Objective was political representation for non-Brahmins.

title: C. N. Annadurai
provisions: Led to formation of Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK).

title: Rashid Ahmad Gangohi
provisions: Advocated revival of orthodox Islamic teachings.

title: Muhammad Qasim Nanautavi
provisions: Advocated revival of orthodox Islamic teachings.

title: Mirza Ghulam Ahmad
provisions: Claimed to be the awaited Mahdi.
Called for respect towards all religions.

title: Baba Dayal Das
provisions: Opposed worship of tombs and graves.

title: Dadabhai Naoroji
provisions: Objective was removal of orthodox superstitions.
